name: Can I sue my boss for lung problems from chemical fumes at work in Ogden?
description: No direct lawsuit against employer in Utah—use workers' comp; sue third parties if applicable.[3]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my boss for lung problems from chemical fumes at work in Ogden?

acceptedAnswer.text: No direct lawsuit against employer in Utah—use workers' comp; sue third parties if applicable.[3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, respiratory injuries from workplace exposure are typically handled through **workers' compensation as the exclusive remedy**, not lawsuits against employers. You can sue third parties like equipment manufacturers if their defect caused exposure.[1][3]

Workers' comp provides no-fault benefits for medical care and lost wages without proving negligence. Exceptions are rare, requiring proof of employer fraud or intentional harm.[3]

**Key factors:**
- Diagnosis linking illness to job exposure
- Employer's failure to provide PPE or ventilation
- No dual capacity or gross negligence piercing exclusivity
- Third-party liability for defective gear

**Practical guidance:** File a workers' comp claim promptly via Utah Labor Commission; hire an attorney for complex exposure cases. Document everything medically.
Consult a specialist to evaluate all options.
